overview: Boros Leyline Aggro is a fast, aggressive Standard deck that aims to overwhelm opponents with early combat damage and efficient creature plays. It leverages low-cost threats and combat tricks to close games before midrange or control strategies can stabilize. The deck relies on consistent curve plays and aggressive sequencing to maintain board presence.
win_conditions: Deal 20 combat damage quickly using low-cost creatures
Utilize combat tricks to bypass blockers or deal excess damage
Maintain board pressure to force opponent into unfavorable trades

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two 1-drops or a 1-drop and a 2-drop
Hands containing early combat tricks or pump effects
Hands that can play a creature on turn 1 and follow up on turn 2
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (duals/shock preferred) plus at least two of: Slickshot Show-Off, Emberheart Challenger, Voice of Victory, or cheap instants (Lightning Helix/Shock).
Keep 2 lands if paired with two 1-drops or a 1-drop plus an instant.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early land drops or creature plays
Hands with multiple removal spells but no early threats
Mulligan on 4+ lands with no threats, or 3+ lands with only one threat.

early_game: Play lands and 1-drops consistently to establish early board presence
Use combat tricks to maximize damage on turns 1 and 2
Avoid trading up unless it leaves you with a larger threat
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Slickshot Show-Off.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Emberheart Challenger or another threat.
If the opponent plays a 2-power or less creature, flash in Voice of Victory to remove it or protect your board.
Sequence lands to guarantee red mana for Emberheart and instants, and use Slickshot's graveyard trigger to cast removal or pump spells for tempo.
mid_game: Deploy signature creatures like Emberheart Challenger to apply pressure
Use Voice of Victory to swing for lethal or clear blockers
Maintain mana advantage by playing creatures before removal resolves
Preserve instant-speed interaction for critical turns rather than burning it on low-impact threats.
Use Slickshot Show-Off to cast graveyard instants for tempo or removal, but avoid overcommitting to graveyard recursion if the opponent has graveyard hate.
Track hand size to ensure you can answer sweepers or counters when needed.
Commit threats when you have removal for their answers or when the board is clear of sweepers.
Hold back if you suspect a board wipe, heavy counter suite, or hand disruption.
Use flash to respond to removal on your key threats, and trade efficiently to maintain board presence without overextending.
late_game: Push for lethal with remaining creatures and combat tricks
Use pump effects to bypass late-game board wipes or sweepers
If stalled, look for value triggers or combat damage to close the game
Emberheart Challenger (often 4/4+ with flying tokens), Slickshot Show-Off + instant pump/removal, and Voice of Victory as a surprise attacker or blocker.
Close with unblockable flyers or pumped ground pounders when the opponent has no answers.
Go all-in when the opponent has no removal, sweepers, or counterspells in hand or graveyard.
Commit when you can swing for lethal or force a trade that leaves you ahead in board state.
Push damage when you know they cannot stabilize or draw into a sweep.

key_cards.name: Voice of Victory
key_cards.notes: Use to swing for lethal or protect key attackers from removal; hold up for combat tricks rather than playing it as a static threat.

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to establish board presence; use its combat ability to trade favorably or push damage through.

key_cards.name: Emberheart Challenger
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn 2 or 3 to maintain aggression; prioritize attacking over blocking to keep pressure on opponent's life total.

sequencing_tips: Play creatures before holding up mana for combat tricks to avoid being outdrawn
Use pump effects on the last creature in combat to maximize damage
Hold removal for opponent's key threats rather than trading early
Prioritize attacking with multiple creatures to force difficult blocker assignments
Slickshot Show-Off's graveyard trigger is conditional ('this turn'), so plan accordingly and avoid wasting it on low-impact spells.
Voice of Victory's 'power 2 or less' restriction means it won't kill most mid-game threats, so use it strategically for flash protection or early removal.
Emberheart Challenger scales with dragons, so include a few cheap dragons if possible.
Avoid overextending into sweepers and always track opponent hand size to know when to commit or hold back.

matchups.advice: Push early damage relentlessly and use combat tricks to bypass counterspells; avoid trading creatures unnecessarily.
Race before they stabilize with removal and big creatures; use pump effects to swing for lethal on turn 3 or 4.
Play for board control and favorable trades; use combat tricks to push damage through their blockers.
Disrupt their setup with early pressure and removal; race if they cannot interact with your board.

Use flash (Voice of Victory) to cast after counterspells resolve. Split threats across turns to force multiple counters. Use Slickshot to cast instants from the graveyard to bypass counters, and avoid overcommitting to a single turn.

Apply maximum early pressure to prevent them from hitting 4+ lands. Use removal on their mana sources if possible, and don't let them stabilize. Trade efficiently to keep them below lethal while you close the game.

Value Slickshot Show-Off and Voice of Victory for tempo. Prioritize graveyard recursion and efficient trades. Use Emberheart to out-value their board and win combat steps by leveraging flying tokens and +1/+1 counters.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Izzet), sweepers (Mono-Green/Selesnya), and hand disruption.
Note if the opponent saves removal for your finishers or if they are holding up mana for a specific answer.
Track graveyard contents for Slickshot triggers and be aware of when they run out of removal, signaling a window to go all-in.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype name and color identity imply aggressive early-game pressure.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Standard aggro principles applied to Boros creature suite.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: General Standard aggro sequencing principles applied to Boros combat suite.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard meta threats that typically counter aggressive creature decks.
